steps.title: Identify the key contrast in the notes
steps.content: The notes contain a specific contrast: Merino and Rambouillet sheep produce fine wool for apparel, while Romney sheep produce thick wool for rugs and blankets. To emphasize how Romney differs, the answer must use these concrete details from the notes.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
C presents this exact contrast—fine wool for apparel versus thick wool for rugs and blankets—directly showing how Romney differs from the other breeds.

A and B acknowledge differences vaguely without specifying what they are; D focuses on economic importance rather than the breed-specific distinction.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
